engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Laguna Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Laguna in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. Every rebuilt unit includes upgraded components addressing known failure points.

cards.altText: Renault Laguna F9Q engine - diesel 1.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade from 70k miles, causing rough idle and power loss.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts exhaust flow, triggering warning lights and reduced performance.
Turbo actuator failure - variable vane mechanism sticks, causing overboost or limp mode on higher-mileage units.

cards.altText: Renault Laguna M9R engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo actuator electrical failure - actuator motor or position sensor fails, causing loss of boost and warning lights.
DPF regeneration issues - frequent short journeys prevent regeneration, leading to clogging and reduced power.
Timing chain tensioner wear - tensioner weakens after 100k miles, risking chain stretch and valve timing issues.

cards.altText: Renault Laguna F4R engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure - belt slip or jump on high-mileage units if service intervals are missed.
Coil pack failure - individual ignition coils fail, causing misfires, rough running and check engine lights.
Oil consumption increase - valve stem seals wear after 80k miles, leading to blue smoke and top-up needs.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Laguna Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Laguna across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The F9Q-700 powered early Laguna II models, bringing common-rail injection and strong low-end torque for executive driving.
commonFailures: Injector seal wear - causes diesel dilution in oil and rough running after 60k miles.
EGR valve sticking - carbon buildup restricts flow, triggering limp mode and warning lights.
Turbo vane actuator failure - variable geometry mechanism seizes, causing boost loss.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q-700 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Updated F9Q variant with refined injection mapping, standard on mid-to-late Laguna II diesel models.
commonFailures: DPF clogging on FAP versions - short journeys prevent regeneration, causing power loss.
Injector calibration drift - uneven fuel delivery leads to misfires and increased emissions.
Turbo oil feed restriction - sludge buildup starves turbo bearings, causing premature wear.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q-760 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Final evolution of the 1.9 dCi for Laguna II, featuring improved emissions hardware for Euro 4 compliance.
commonFailures: EGR cooler leakage - coolant enters exhaust system, causing white smoke and overheating.
Swirl flap actuator failure - plastic linkage breaks, triggering fault codes and reduced efficiency.
High-pressure fuel pump wear - internal wear reduces rail pressure, causing hard starting.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q-762 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Laguna III's flagship diesel, delivering strong performance and improved refinement over the 1.9 dCi.
commonFailures: Turbo actuator electrical fault - position sensor or motor fails, causing limp mode and boost loss.
Timing chain tensioner wear - tensioner weakens after 100k miles, risking chain stretch.
EGR valve carbon buildup - restricted flow triggers warning lights and reduced power.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R-700 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output M9R variant for GT and Initiale trims, featuring revised turbo mapping for 175hp output.
compatibleVariants: GT, Initiale, GT 4Control, Sport Tourer GT (2008-2015)
commonFailures: Turbo overboost protection activation - aggressive mapping stresses turbo, triggering limp mode.
DPF regeneration failure - frequent short trips prevent burn-off, causing clogging and power loss.
Injector calibration drift - uneven fuel delivery causes rough running and increased emissions.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R-744 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Late-production M9R with AdBlue-compatible hardware for Euro 5 emissions compliance in urban zones.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique Euro 5, Initiale Euro 5, Sport Tourer Euro 5 (2010-2015)
commonFailures: AdBlue injector clogging - crystallisation blocks dosing system, triggering emissions warnings.
NOx sensor failure - sensor degradation causes false emissions readings and limp mode.
DPF pressure sensor fault - inaccurate readings prevent regeneration, causing clogging.
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R Euro 5 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable naturally-aspirated petrol unit powering entry and mid-spec Laguna II models with smooth delivery.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear - tensioner weakens, risking belt slip and valve damage if missed.
Ignition coil failure - individual coils fail causing misfires, rough running and check engine light.
Oil consumption increase - valve stem seals wear after 80k miles, leading to blue smoke.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F4R-700 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Updated F4R with refined ECU mapping for improved emissions and fuel economy on late Laguna II.
commonFailures: Variable valve timing solenoid failure - VVT system sticks, causing rough idle and power loss.
Carbon buildup on intake valves - direct injection models suffer reduced airflow and efficiency.
Thermostat housing leak - plastic housing cracks, causing coolant loss and overheating risk.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F4R-764 Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Turbocharged F4R variant delivering strong performance for Dynamique and GT Laguna II models.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique Turbo, GT, Sport Tourer Turbo (2005-2007)
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate actuator failure - actuator sticks causing overboost or underboost and limp mode.
Intercooler pipe splitting - heat cycling cracks plastic pipes, causing boost loss and whistling.
High-pressure fuel pump wear - internal wear reduces rail pressure, causing hard starting under load.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F4Rt Renault Laguna engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Economical entry-level petrol engine for budget-conscious Laguna II buyers, offering reliable daily driving.
commonFailures: Camshaft position sensor failure - sensor degradation causes hard starting and stalling.
Oil leak from valve cover gasket - gasket hardens with age, causing oil seepage onto exhaust.
Coolant thermostat sticking - thermostat fails open or closed, affecting warm-up and efficiency.
entries.cta: Get quotes for K4M-720 Renault Laguna engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Renault Laguna Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Laguna's 1.9 dCi (F9Q) and 2.0 dCi (M9R) diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with injector, EGR and turbo issues most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to compare repair vs replacement costs for your Laguna.

problems.h4: Renault Laguna 1.9 dCi Injector Failure - Why F9Q Engines Develop Power Loss
problems.affectedModels: Renault Laguna Expression, Dynamique, Initiale 1.9 dCi (2001-2007, F9Q-700/760/762)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure piezo injectors degrade internally, causing uneven fuel delivery, diesel dilution in oil and rough running.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Injector Service - Clean and recalibrate existing injectors
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, ultrasonic clean, flow test and recalibrate on bench
repairOptions.longevity: May restore performance temporarily; not viable if injectors are physically worn

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Injector Replacement - Replace faulty injectors with reconditioned units
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace 1-4 injectors with reconditioned units, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Good solution if block is healthy; addresses immediate symptoms but doesn't fix underlying wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt F9Q Engine - Complete engine replacement with upgraded injectors
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value if engine has other wear; eliminates injector risk and restores like-new reliability

problems.recommendation: If your Laguna has under 90k miles and the block is healthy, injector replacement offers good value. For higher-mileage examples or if oil dilution is severe, a rebuilt F9Q engine with upgraded injectors is more cost-effective long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt F9Q engine prices for your Renault Laguna

problems.group: Renault Laguna 2.0 dCi Turbo Actuator Failure
problems.h4: Renault Laguna 2.0 dCi Turbo Actuator Failure - Why M9R Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Renault Laguna Dynamique, Initiale, GT 2.0 dCi (2007-2015, M9R-700/744)
problems.rootCause: Turbo actuator motor or position sensor fails electrically, preventing variable vane control and triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Actuator Reset/Calibration - Software reset and actuator relearn
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, actuator calibration routine, road test to verify boost control
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if actuator is mechanically sound; temporary fix if electrical fault is intermittent

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Actuator Replacement - Replace faulty actuator assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo actuator, fit new or reconditioned unit, calibrate and road test
repairOptions.longevity: Good solution if turbo core is healthy; addresses limp mode but doesn't fix internal turbo wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M9R Engine - Complete engine replacement with upgraded turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo assembly, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if turbo has internal wear or engine has other issues; eliminates actuator risk and restores full performance

problems.recommendation: For early-stage actuator faults with a healthy turbo, replacement offers good value. If boost pressure is inconsistent or turbo noise is present, a rebuilt M9R engine with upgraded turbo is the more reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for M9R Renault Laguna engine replacement

problems.group: Renault Laguna 1.9 dCi EGR Valve Clogging
problems.h4: Renault Laguna 1.9 dCi EGR Valve Clogging - Why Power Drops and Warning Lights Appear
problems.affectedModels: Renault Laguna 1.9 dCi all trims (2001-2007, F9Q series)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve accumulates carbon deposits, restricting flow and causing incorrect air-fuel ratios.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cleaning - Remove and clean valve and passages
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove EGR valve, clean carbon deposits, clean intake passages, refit and test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early-stage clogging; may need repeating if driving pattern doesn't change

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Valve Replacement - Fit new or reconditioned valve
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR valve assembly, clear fault codes, road test to verify operation
repairOptions.longevity: Good solution if valve is physically damaged; addresses immediate symptoms but carbon can rebuild

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt F9Q Engine - Complete engine with upgraded emissions hardware
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear or high mileage; eliminates EGR risk and restores emissions compliance

problems.recommendation: Cleaning is cost-effective for early symptoms. If the valve is seized or the engine has other issues, a rebuilt F9Q engine offers better long-term reliability and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare F9Q engine replacement prices for your Renault Laguna

problems.h4: Renault Laguna Electrical/ECU Issues - Why Laguna II Models Develop Starting Problems
problems.affectedModels: Renault Laguna II all engines (2001-2007, early keyless systems)
problems.rootCause: Early keyless entry/ignition system and engine management ECU can develop electrical faults causing no-start or intermittent running.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - ECU Diagnostic/Reset - Scan and reset fault codes
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full diagnostic scan, clear fault codes, test keyless system and immobiliser
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable for intermittent electrical glitches; not a fix for hardware failure

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - ECU/Immobiliser Repair - Repair or replace faulty control unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove ECU/immobiliser, repair or replace unit, reprogram to vehicle, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Good solution if fault is isolated to control unit; doesn't address other electrical aging

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Updated Wiring - Complete engine with refreshed harness
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with updated wiring loom, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if multiple electrical issues exist; eliminates ECU risk and restores reliable starting

problems.recommendation: For isolated ECU faults, repair offers good value. If multiple electrical issues exist or the vehicle has high mileage, a rebuilt engine with updated wiring provides more comprehensive reliability.
problems.cta: Find Renault Laguna engine specialists for electrical fault diagnosis

problems.group: Renault Laguna 2.0 Petrol Timing Belt Failure
problems.h4: Renault Laguna 2.0 Petrol Timing Belt Failure - Why Preventative Replacement Matters
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner weakens with age; if belt slips or breaks, valves collide with pistons causing catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Timing Belt Kit Replacement - Replace belt, tensioner, water pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers and water pump per service schedule
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done on schedule

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Repair - Repair bent valves after belt failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace bent valves, reassemble and test compression
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if piston/cylinder damage is minimal; expensive and doesn't guarantee long-term reliability

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt F4R Engine - Complete engine replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit rebuilt engine with new timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value after catastrophic failure; eliminates timing risk and restores like-new reliability

problems.recommendation: Always replace the timing belt kit at recommended intervals. If failure has occurred, a rebuilt F4R engine is more cost-effective and reliable than valve repair on a damaged block.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt F4R petrol engine prices for your Renault Laguna

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Laguna
finalCta.paragraph: From injector wear on the 1.9 dCi to turbo actuator faults on the 2.0 dCi, Renault Laguna engine problems are common but solvable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Laguna specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components addressing the original failure point. With nationwide delivery, supply & fit options, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Laguna Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Laguna and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Renault Laguna Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Laguna's UK production timeline (2001-2015), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices.

years.preview: Laguna II Launch: F9Q Diesel & F4R Petrol Debut
years.description: The second-generation Laguna launched with the F9Q 1.9 dCi diesel and F4R 2.0 petrol, establishing the core engine lineup for UK executive buyers.
keyChanges: Laguna II replaces first generation with new platform and keyless entry system
F9Q common-rail diesel introduced with 107-120hp outputs for fleet appeal
F4R 2.0 16V petrol offered as smoother, simpler alternative to diesel
Early models used F9Q-700 and F4R-700 engine codes
knownFor: Strong executive appeal with comfortable ride and refined diesel performance
Early keyless entry system reliability concerns on some Laguna II examples
F9Q injector wear becoming apparent on higher-mileage fleet vehicles
F4R timing belt maintenance critical to avoid catastrophic valve damage
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2004 Renault Laguna replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Laguna 1.9 dCi injector replacement • Renault Laguna 2.0 petrol timing belt cost • Renault Laguna engine won't start diagnosis

years.preview: Laguna II Facelift: F9Q-760 & FAP Emissions Hardware
years.description: The facelifted Laguna II introduced updated F9Q-760 engines with FAP particulate filters for improved emissions compliance in urban zones.
keyChanges: F9Q-760 and F9Q-762 engine codes introduced with 130hp output
FAP (particulate filter) hardware added to some diesel variants for Euro 4
F4R-764 petrol variant with refined ECU mapping for better economy
TomTom Edition trim launched with integrated navigation
knownFor: Improved refinement and emissions compliance on facelift models
FAP regeneration issues on short-journey cars causing power loss
F9Q-762 injector calibration drift becoming common failure point
Strong UK replacement demand on Dynamique and Initiale diesel variants

years.preview: Laguna III Launch: M9R 2.0 dCi & Refined F4R
years.description: The third-generation Laguna introduced the M9R 2.0 dCi diesel as the new flagship, with improved refinement but new turbo actuator concerns.
keyChanges: Laguna III platform launched with new styling and interior technology
M9R 2.0 dCi diesel replaces F9Q as primary diesel option (150-175hp)
F4R petrol continued with minor updates for emissions compliance
Coupe body style introduced alongside hatchback and Sport Tourer
knownFor: Significant refinement improvement over Laguna II with quieter cabin
M9R turbo actuator electrical faults emerging as common failure point
DPF regeneration requirements more critical on Euro 5 compliant units
Strong executive appeal but higher replacement cost than F9Q engines

M9R Euro 5 variants with AdBlue-compatible hardware for urban zones
Final production year 2015; no direct successor in Renault lineup
Increased focus on ULEZ compliance for London and other clean air zones

M9R-701 - updated 2.0 diesel for late-production models
F4R final production - 2.0 petrol for remaining stock

faq.h2: Renault Laguna Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Laguna engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Renault Laguna engine prices range from £750 for a 1.6L petrol to £2,200 for a 2.0 dCi diesel, supply only. Supply & fit adds £350-£650 labour depending on location and workshop.
keyPoints: F9Q 1.9 dCi rebuilt engines typically £950-£1,800 supply only
M9R 2.0 dCi rebuilt engines typically £1,200-£2,200 supply only
F4R 2.0 petrol rebuilt engines typically £850-£1,600 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £350-£650 to engine price
All prices include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Renault Laguna engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit the Renault Laguna?
items.answer: Main Renault Laguna engine codes are F9Q (1.9 dCi diesel), M9R (2.0 dCi diesel), F4R (2.0 petrol) and K4M (1.6 petrol), with suffix numbers indicating power output and emissions spec.
keyPoints: M9R-700/744: 2.0 dCi diesel 150-175hp, Laguna III 2007-2015
K4M-720: 1.6 petrol 110hp, entry-level trims 2001-2007
Always verify code via VIN or engine plate before ordering replacement
items.cta: Find your Renault Laguna engine code with free VIN check

items.question: Is a reconditioned Renault Laguna engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, reconditioned Renault Laguna engines from vetted UK specialists are reliable when rebuilt to OEM spec with upgraded components addressing known failure points like injectors or turbos.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines undergo strip, clean, inspection and parts replacement
Upgraded injectors, turbos or tensioners address model-specific weaknesses
Dyno-tested before dispatch with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Choose suppliers offering compression test reports and mileage verification
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Renault Laguna engine suppliers UK

items.question: How long does Renault Laguna engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: Typical Renault Laguna engine supply & fit takes 2-4 days from order to collection, including engine dispatch, fitting, ECU coding and road testing at a partner garage.
keyPoints: Engine dispatch usually 24-48 hours from order confirmation
Fitting takes 1 day at partner garage with pre-booked slot
ECU coding and road testing completed before vehicle collection
Nationwide garage network covers most UK postcodes for convenience
items.cta: Book Renault Laguna engine supply & fit appointment today

items.question: Can I replace my Renault Laguna 1.9 dCi engine with a 2.0 dCi?
items.answer: Swapping a 1.9 dCi for a 2.0 dCi in a Renault Laguna is possible but requires ECU reprogramming, wiring modifications and emissions compliance checks - not recommended without specialist support.
keyPoints: M9R 2.0 dCi uses different ECU and wiring harness than F9Q 1.9 dCi
ECU reprogramming and immobiliser matching required for swap to work
Emissions compliance may be affected; verify ULEZ/CAZ rules before swap
Specialist engine fitters can advise on feasibility and additional costs
items.cta: Enquire about Renault Laguna engine swap compatibility with specialists

items.question: What is the most common Renault Laguna engine problem?
items.answer: The most common Renault Laguna engine problem is injector wear on the 1.9 dCi F9Q engine, causing rough running, power loss and diesel dilution in oil after 70k-120k miles.
keyPoints: F9Q injector wear causes uneven fuel delivery and rough idle
EGR valve clogging and turbo actuator faults also frequent on F9Q/M9R diesels
F4R petrol engines commonly suffer timing belt tensioner wear if not serviced
Early diagnosis and preventative maintenance can avoid catastrophic failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Laguna injector or turbo replacement

items.question: Does Renault Laguna engine replacement include warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every Renault Laguna engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at any UK workshop.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK VAT-registered garage
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options for additional cost
Always confirm exact warranty terms with chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare Renault Laguna engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How do I find my Renault Laguna engine code?
items.answer: Find your Renault Laguna engine code on the engine identification plate (usually on timing cover or cylinder head), in the V5C logbook, or by entering your registration for instant DVLA lookup.
keyPoints: Engine plate typically shows code like F9Q-760 or M9R-700
V5C logbook section V.5 may list engine code or displacement
DVLA registration lookup instantly confirms engine code and variant
Take photo of engine plate when ordering replacement to avoid errors
items.cta: Use free Renault Laguna engine code lookup tool now

items.question: Is a used Renault Laguna engine worth buying?
items.answer: A used Renault Laguna engine can be worth buying if sourced from a reputable breaker with mileage verification, compression test report and warranty, offering budget replacement for older vehicles.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £750-£1,600 vs £950-£2,200 for rebuilt units
Request mileage confirmation and compression test before purchase
Ensure compatibility with your variant's ECU and emissions hardware
Budget option best for older Lagunas or tight-budget replacements
items.cta: Check used Renault Laguna engine availability with verified mileage

items.question: Can you deliver a Renault Laguna engine nationwide UK?
items.answer: Yes, Renault Laguna engines are delivered nationwide UK via courier or specialist transport, with next-day delivery available on many rebuilt units and collection service offered by some suppliers.
keyPoints: Most suppliers offer next-day UK mainland delivery on rebuilt engines
Larger engines may require specialist transport; confirm cost before order
Collection service available at some supplier depots to save delivery fees
Engine packaged securely with lifting points and protection for transit
